title: J.P. Morgan India Capital Markets Initiation: AngelOne > CAMS > ICICIAMC (Jun 2026)
stale_items: Entire report — this is the identical J.P. Morgan India Capital Markets initiation already processed on 2026-06-23
duplicate_stories: J.P. Morgan initiates India Capital Markets coverage: AngelOne > CAMS > ICICIAMC > NAM > HDFCAMC preferred picks; Neutral BSE/KFINTECH; UW CDSL/MCX
SIP-led financialization thesis: monthly SIPs Rs310bn (+48% YoY) despite flat Nifty and US$36bn FPI outflows over FY25-26
TER scale-based regulation (FY27) capping AMC operating leverage
BSE index-options market share gain to ~34% and dynamic P/E valuation framework
MCX bullion/energy volume surge (FY26 futures ADTV +138%) seen as cyclically peaked
executive_summary: J.P. Morgan initiates India Capital Markets coverage anchored on SIP-led financialization: monthly industry SIP flows up 48% to Rs310bn (May-26), 77% of FY26 equity+balanced net inflows, despite Nifty flat (2yr CAGR 0.8% in Rs) and FPIs selling US$36bn over FY25-26; cumulative equity/balanced net inflows Rs9.43tn.

Preferred order AngelOne > CAMS > ICICIAMC > NAM > HDFCAMC (all OW); Neutral BSE & KFINTECH (downgraded from OW); UW CDSL & MCX. Thesis invalidated if monthly SIPs fall below Rs250bn sustained or regulation cuts derivative volumes >20%.

Sub-segment valuation hierarchy: Exchanges > Depositories > Brokers > AMCs > MF RTAs, driven by durability of the earnings pool — value-linked exchange monetisation and inflation-linked pricing at top, B2B yield compression at MF RTAs at bottom.

AngelOne is top pick — cheapest multiple (FY28E P/E 19.8x) with fastest EPS growth (FY26-28E CAGR 36%), best-in-class PEG 0.7x; 4Q26 order inflection (431mn, +13.3% QoQ) marks first positive print post-SEBI F&O curbs; PT Rs420, ~18% upside.

AMC pecking order ICICIAMC (RoE ~83-86%, 14.2% equity share, 17.8% net inflow share) > NAM (bullion/ETF leader, 21.4% passive share, fastest-growing top-10 AMC) > HDFCAMC (lowest opex 10bps AAUM but slowest EPS growth 15.3%); all face 3-4bps gross TER headwind from Apr-2026.

CAMS preferred MF RTA (~68% share, Rs55.1tn AuM) on non-MF diversification (14.3% of revenue, targeting Rs4bn by FY29) and AI-led ReArch cost program lifting EBITDA margin to 50% by FY28; KFINTECH downgraded on Ascent integration margin drag (FY26 EBITDA -320bps to 40.7%, troughing ~39% FY27E).

Exchanges re-rated on delivery not multiples: BSE index-options ADPTV Rs289bn (+104%), ~34% share, EBIT margin 60.4%, but Neutral as near-term growth priced in (FY28E P/E 42.7x); MCX UW as bullion 'gold rush' priced in (38x FY28E P/E, PEG 1.7x) with volumes seen peaking.

CDSL UW despite 80.2% demat dominance and multi-decade penetration runway — highest PEG (4.5x) meets slowest EPS CAGR (12.9%); FY28E P/E 49.3x, DCF PT Rs1,200 implies ~13% downside; depositories flagged as most cyclical given cash-delivery/IPO exposure with no derivatives offset.

key_takeaways.takeaway: SIP flows have decoupled from equity returns, giving AMC/RTA revenue pools annuity-like resilience even through a 20-month flat-market patch.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: Monthly SIPs at Rs310bn (+48% YoY) with 108mn accounts and stable ~Rs3,000 ticket sustained despite Nifty flat and US$36bn FPI outflows demonstrates sticky, granular, non-cyclical cash generation for AMCs and MF RTAs. Track monthly SIP run-rate: a sustained fall below Rs250bn is the explicit thesis-invalidation trigger for the entire AUM-linked complex.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Scale-based TER regulation structurally caps AMC operating leverage — larger AMCs face steeper fee compression, inverting the usual 'scale = margin' logic.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: The FY27 base-TER reset (excluding statutory levies) plus brokerage cuts (cash 12→5bps, derivatives 5→2bps) create a 3-4bps gross yield headwind. This rewards operational efficiency (HDFCAMC opex at 10bps AAUM absorbs best) over pricing power. Track blended MF yield trajectory and whether cost programs offset the pass-through to distributors; a fifteen-year regulatory arc has consistently favoured cost reduction and transparency.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Exchange and MCX earnings have been re-rated on delivery, not multiple expansion — leaving them hostage to cyclically elevated volatility and commodity prices.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: Near-perfect alignment between FY21-26 earnings CAGR and stock returns (BSE APAT +82%/price +110%, MCX +43%/+57%) shows valuations already reflect fundamentals. BSE's monthly index-option notional share approaching structural ~50% ceiling and MCX trading above +2SD P/BV mean limited headroom if volatility normalises. Track BSE options ADPTV (Rs270bn floor for re-rating, Rs250bn invalidation) and MCX futures ADTV sustainability above Rs750bn.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens_metric: BSE index-option ADPTV (Rs bn) / MCX futures ADTV (Rs bn)

key_takeaways.takeaway: The cyclicality ranking inverts conventional franchise hierarchy — CDSL, despite an oligopolistic demat moat, screens as the MOST cyclical due to concentrated cash-delivery and IPO exposure with no derivatives offset.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: CDSL earnings hinge on IPO pipeline, account-opening momentum and KRA pricing rather than the broader value-based derivatives complex. FY26 transaction revenue fell 9.8% and IPO income fell 1.9%; the highest PEG in coverage (4.5x) against slowest EPS CAGR (12.9%) and 49.3x FY28E P/E leaves the structural story fully priced. Track quarterly IPO issue-size conversion and incremental demat account share (currently ~85%).

key_takeaways.takeaway: MF RTAs are structural price-takers under telescopic fee architecture — a permanent valuation discount that only non-MF diversification can defend.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: CAMS/KFINTECH blended yields decline mechanically as AUM scales (CAMS 3.4→1.9bps by FY29E; KFIN 3.9→3.3bps), a sector-wide structural feature, not stock-specific. CAMS defends via non-MF (targeting 16% of revenue by FY29) and AI cost control (EBITDA 45%→50% FY28E); KFINTECH's Ascent acquisition adds international optionality but compresses margins near-term. Track non-MF revenue mix and RTA blended realisation per Rs bn of AUM.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens_metric: RTA blended yield (bps) and non-MF revenue share (%)

key_takeaways.takeaway: AngelOne offers the cleanest growth-adjusted risk-reward but is levered to F&O regulatory/volatility cycle rather than broad market direction.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: OW thesis rests on 4Q26 order inflection (431mn, +13.3% QoQ) being a durable regulatory reset post-SEBI F&O curbs, not a transient cyclical uptick. MTF book Rs54.5bn (35.8% FY20-26 CAGR) makes interest income 32% of gross revenue, adding annuity ballast. Cheapest coverage multiple (19.8x FY28E) at 35% discount to Groww's ~35x. Track daily order run-rate sustaining above 7mn and any move to single-day/eliminated weekly expiry.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens_metric: daily order run-rate (mn) and F&O revenue share of broking

key_takeaways.takeaway: Near-term OW calls are explicitly predicated on constructive market momentum — the sector's high beta means a weaker regime could hurt returns even on preferred names.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: J.P. Morgan itself caveats that rankings and PT upside are hard to sustain without upward drift in prices/earnings. This is a directional market call as much as a stock-selection framework. Investors should size positions for near-term beta risk; the medium-term financialization framework stands but is not immune to a volatility shock (the report was published amid West Asia conflict / Operation Sindoor volatility context).
key_takeaways.analyst_lens_metric: India VIX and Nifty 50 total return

entities_impacted.impact: Top pick; OW PT Rs420. Post-F&O-curb order recovery, scaling MTF interest income (32% of revenue) and Super App diversification drive FY26-28E PAT CAGR 36%; cheapest PEG (0.7x) in coverage but high F&O/regulatory beta.
Preferred MF RTA; OW PT Rs950. ~68% share, non-MF diversification and AI-led cost control lift EBITDA margin to 50% by FY28, offsetting structural telescopic yield compression.
Top AMC pick; OW PT Rs4,090. Highest equity share 14.2%, net inflow share 17.8%, standout RoE ~83-86%, lean 14bps cost ratio; TER headwind 3-4bps gross but management confident of mitigation.
OW PT Rs1,360. Fastest-growing top-10 AMC (+63bps share to 8.89%), bullion ETF leader (Gold AUM Rs533bn +199%), 21.4% passive share; most levered to current cycle drivers.
OW PT raised to Rs3,250. Lowest opex (10bps AAUM) best absorbs TER reset but slowest EPS growth (15.3%) caps re-rating; net inflow share risen 590bps to 13.9%.
Neutral PT Rs4,330. Index-options share ~34%, ADPTV +104%, EBIT margin 60.4%; franchise liked but near-term growth priced in (42.7x FY28E) and re-rating amplified by peak volatility.

Downgraded OW→Neutral, PT cut Rs1,200→Rs980. Ascent acquisition dilutes FY26 EBITDA margin -320bps to 40.7%, troughing ~39% FY27E before recovery; international optionality offset by near-term margin drag.
UW PT Rs1,200 (~13% downside). 80.2% demat dominance but highest PEG (4.5x) meets slowest EPS CAGR (12.9%); most cyclical in framework given cash-delivery/IPO exposure; 49.3x FY28E P/E.
UW PT Rs2,560 (~11% downside). >98% commodity derivatives share but bullion/energy 'gold rush' priced in; volumes seen peaked, 38x FY28E P/E vs 34x target, PEG 1.7x.
Central swing factor — TER reforms (base-TER Apr-26, brokerage cuts), F&O expiry standardisation, position-limit changes drive earnings across AMCs, exchanges and brokers.
OW PT Rs220; superior revenue/margin profile (PAT margin ~45-50%, FY26-28E orders CAGR 25%) used as valuation benchmark — AngelOne trades at 35% discount to Groww's ~35x.
MF AUM projected to Rs325tn by FY35E (equity 20% CAGR); structurally less cyclical (AUM-linked) but TER scale regulation caps operating leverage industry-wide.
Household financial savings rising (gross 63% of household savings FY24 vs 52% FY14); MF share of financial assets 8%→12% (FY22-25); market-cap/GDP 136% with runway vs DM peers.

learning: Cyclicality is defined by the BASIS of monetisation, not by market structure or moat width. CDSL's oligopoly looks defensive but its revenue is tied to cash-delivery volumes and IPO issuance with no derivatives diversification — making it more cyclical than 'competitive' brokers. When assessing earnings durability, map revenue to its underlying driver (value-linked vs per-transaction vs per-order) before assuming a monopoly equals stability.

A low headline multiple is not automatically cheap and a high one not automatically expensive — the PEG lens (P/E ÷ EPS CAGR) reconciles them. AngelOne at 19.8x with 36% growth (PEG 0.7x) is cheaper on a growth-adjusted basis than CDSL at 49.3x with 12.9% growth (PEG 4.5x). For rating durability, always growth-adjust and cross-check against RoE and reinvestment needs.

Regulation that 'passes economies of scale to investors' (telescopic TER, base-TER resets) structurally caps operating leverage in an otherwise asset-light model — the scale advantage accrues to customers, not shareholders. When a fifteen-year regulatory trajectory consistently favours cost reduction, model the fee pool as a declining take-rate and reward operational efficiency over pricing power.

Sell-side ranking frameworks embedded in a directional market call carry hidden beta risk — J.P. Morgan explicitly flags that its OW upside 'may be difficult to sustain' without upward price/earnings drift. Treat medium-term structural theses and near-term tactical positioning as separate questions; a sound franchise view does not protect against a volatility-regime reversal.
